intitule: Vocabulary Match: Interacting with Peers from Other Cultures

enonce.texte: Match the words on the left with their correct definitions on the right to review key vocabulary related to interacting with peers from other tribes and cultures.

questions.texte: Match the following terms with their definitions.

sousQuestions.reponse: d. The state of having people from different cultures, backgrounds, or identities.
a. The process of combining different groups into a unified whole.
b. The ability to accept and respect differences in others.
c. The customs, beliefs, and way of life of a particular group of people.
e. A person who is of equal status, age, or ability to another.

intitule: Using Adjuncts: Fill in the Blanks

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences below with the correct adjunct from the box: while, for, neither... nor, either... or, only, also, never, ever, although

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the appropriate adjunct.
questions.reponse: 1. while, 2. neither... nor, 3. although, 4. either... or, 5. also, 6. never, 7. for, 8. only

sousQuestions.texte: ______ I was talking to my friend from the North, my other friend from the South joined us.
He speaks ______ Bamiléké ______ Arabic, but he understands both.
______ she is from a different tribe, they became good friends.
You can choose ______ to learn about the culture ______ to share yours.
He is kind and he is ______ respectful to everyone.
I have ______ experienced discrimination, but I always stay positive.
We worked together ______ two hours on the project.
______ you can make a difference by accepting others.

intitule: Reading Comprehension: A Story About Cultural Integration

enonce.texte:
Read the story below carefully and answer the questions that follow.

Title: A New Friend

"Yves is a boy from the Bamiléké tribe in the West region of Cameroon. When he moved to Douala, he felt nervous because he didn't know anyone. At his new school, he met Ahmed, a boy from the Fulani tribe in the North. Although they spoke different languages at home, they discovered that they both loved football. While playing football together, they became close friends. Yves also learned about Fulani traditions, and Ahmed visited Yves' family during cultural celebrations. Neither of them had ever had a friend from a different tribe before. They soon realized that friendship has no boundaries. Yves and Ahmed often say, 'We may come from different tribes, but we are one people.'"

questions.texte: Where did Yves come from originally?
questions.reponse: Yves came from the Bamiléké tribe in the West region of Cameroon.

questions.texte: What activity helped Yves and Ahmed become friends?
questions.reponse: Playing football together helped them become friends.

questions.texte: What did Yves learn about Ahmed's culture?
Had they ever had a friend from a different tribe before?
questions.reponse: No, neither of them had ever had a friend from a different tribe before.

questions.texte: What lesson do Yves and Ahmed want to share?
questions.reponse: They want to share that even though they come from different tribes, they are one people, and friendship has no boundaries.

enonce.texte:
Write a short narrative paragraph (about 100-150 words) about a time when you interacted with a peer from another tribe or culture.

Your paragraph should include:

  • A description of the person (where they come from, their culture)
  • What you did together
  • What you learned from the experience
  • At least two of the following adjuncts: while, although, neither... nor, either... or, also, never, ever

questions.texte: Write a well-structured narrative paragraph using appropriate vocabulary and grammar.
questions.reponse: Students should produce a clear narrative paragraph with a logical structure, proper use of vocabulary related to cultural interaction, and correct use of at least two adjuncts from the lesson.

intitule: Phonetics: Contrasting Vowel and Consonant Sounds

enonce.texte: Read the words below and identify the vowel or consonant sound that is different in each group. Then, choose the correct answer.

questions.texte: Which word has a different vowel sound?

sousQuestions.texte: A. foot / B. good / C. food / D. book
sousQuestions.reponse: C. 'food' has a long /uː/ sound, while the others have a short /ʊ/ sound.

questions.texte: Which word has a different vowel sound?

sousQuestions.texte: A. head / B. bread / C. said / D. great
sousQuestions.reponse: D. 'great' has an /eɪ/ sound, while the others have an /ɛ/ sound.

questions.texte: Which word has a different consonant sound?

sousQuestions.texte: A. thin / B. thank / C. think / D. this
sousQuestions.reponse: D. 'this' has a /ð/ sound, while the others have a /θ/ sound.

questions.texte: Which word has a different consonant sound?

sousQuestions.texte: A. ship / B. shop / C. sure / D. sheep
sousQuestions.reponse: C. 'sure' has a /ʃ/ sound but it is spelled differently; however, all have the /ʃ/ sound. This is a trick question—all have the same consonant sound.

intitule: Writing an Expository Paragraph on Cultural Integration

enonce.texte:
Write an expository paragraph explaining why it is important to interact with peers from other tribes and cultures. Use at least three of the adjuncts studied in this lesson.

Your paragraph should:

  • Have a clear topic sentence
  • Give reasons and examples
  • Conclude with a strong closing statement

questions.texte: Write a well-organized expository paragraph using appropriate vocabulary and grammar.
questions.reponse: Students should produce a clear expository paragraph with a topic sentence, supporting details, and a conclusion. They should correctly use at least three adjuncts such as 'although,' 'while,' 'neither... nor,' 'also,' 'never,' or 'ever'.

intitule: Creating a Dialogue About Cultural Differences

enonce.texte:
Imagine you are meeting a new student from a different tribe at your school. Write a dialogue between you and this new student. Your dialogue should include:

  • A polite greeting
  • Asking about each other's cultures
  • Sharing something about your own culture
  • At least two of the following adjuncts: while, although, neither... nor, either... or, also, never, ever

questions.texte: Write a realistic and well-structured dialogue using appropriate vocabulary and grammar.
questions.reponse: Students should produce a dialogue with proper punctuation, greeting, exchange of cultural information, and correct use of at least two adjuncts from the lesson.
